explanations.1: The four primary tissue types are epithelial, connective, muscle, and nervous. Embryonic refers to a developmental stage or the germ layers (ectoderm, mesoderm, and endoderm) rather than an adult tissue category.
explanations.2: Differentiation is the biological process where unspecialized embryonic cells mature and transform into cells with specific structures and functions.
explanations.3: All differentiated tissues in the body originate from the three embryonic germ layers: the ectoderm, mesoderm, and endoderm.
explanations.4: Mucous membranes (mucosae) line body cavities and digestive, respiratory, excretory, and reproductive tracts that open to the external environment.
explanations.5: Columnar epithelial cells are characterized by being taller than they are wide, with an elongated nucleus usually located toward the basal end of the cell.
explanations.6: The interior of blood vessels is lined by a simple squamous epithelium called endothelium, which provides a thin, smooth surface for efficient fluid flow and gas exchange.
explanations.7: Pseudostratified ciliated columnar epithelium contains cilia on its apical surface that move mucus and trapped particles across the tissue surface, common in the respiratory tract.
explanations.8: Holocrine secretion involves the rupture and destruction of the entire gland cell to release its product, while apocrine secretion involves only the loss of the apical portion of the cell.
explanations.9: Connective tissue is defined by its specialized cells and an extracellular matrix composed of ground substance and protein fibers.
explanations.10: Hyaline cartilage contains chondrocytes located in small clearings called lacunae, set within a smooth, transparent, and glassy matrix.
explanations.11: Adipose tissue is a specialized loose connective tissue consisting mainly of fat-storage cells called adipocytes.
explanations.12: Dense regular connective tissue features collagen fibers packed in parallel bundles, providing the high tensile strength required for ligaments and tendons.
explanations.13: Mesenchyme is the embryonic tissue derived from the mesoderm that serves as the precursor to all connective tissues in the body.
explanations.14: Osteocytes are the mature, primary cells found in bone tissue responsible for maintaining the mineralized matrix; fibroblasts and chondrocytes belong to other connective tissue types.
explanations.15: Skeletal muscle cells are uniquely long, cylindrical, striated, and contain multiple nuclei per cell. Cardiac muscle is also striated but branched and typically has only one or two nuclei.
explanations.16: Myoblasts are the embryonic cells that fuse together to form a mature muscle fiber or myocyte.
explanations.17: Mitochondria are the organelles responsible for ATP production, which is required in high quantities for the intensive energy demands of skeletal muscle contraction.
explanations.18: Neurons are the specialized cells of the nervous system that propagate information via electrochemical impulses, whereas glial cells provide support functions.
explanations.19: The axon is the long cytoplasmic extension of a neuron that conducts nerve impulses away from the cell body toward other target cells.
explanations.20: Astrocytes are star-shaped glial cells in the central nervous system that regulate the chemical environment, neurotransmitter uptake, and the blood-brain barrier.
explanations.21: The four cardinal signs of inflammation are redness (rubor), heat (calor), swelling (tumor), and pain (dolor); fever is a systemic response, not a local cardinal sign.
explanations.22: Mast cells release histamine, a powerful vasodilator that increases blood flow and capillary permeability to initiate the inflammatory response.
explanations.23: Atrophy is the process of tissue or organ shrinkage due to the loss of cell mass or number, often resulting from lack of use or aging.
explanations.24: While lifestyle modifications like diet, exercise, and stress management can influence the aging process, genetic factors are inherited and cannot be changed by the individual.
